stem: A IT operations data backup uses 4 parallel storage devices for redundancy. The data recoverable requires at least one storage device to function. storage device 1 has a reliability of 87%. storage device 2 has a reliability between 78% and 90% (uncertain due to limited operational data). storage device 3 has a reliability of 81%. storage device 4 has a reliability of 88%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all storage devices simultaneously, estimated between 3.0% and 5.7%. What is the overall probability that the data recoverable?

ground_truth.answer: P(data recoverable) ranges from 0.9423 to 0.9697. Point estimate: 0.9565.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 4 storage devices fail simultaneously).
With common-cause failure (3.0%-5.7%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9423, 0.9697].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
